player_notes.jalen williams: Williams was absent for Thunder in Game 4 (per Windhorst concern about OKC playing without him). Monitor availability for Game 5 — his absence significantly hurt OKC's offense.
player_notes.victor wembanyama: Dropped 33 pts, 8 reb, 5 ast, 3 blk in Game 4 win. Trending as the dominant force in this series; high floor across all prop categories for Game 5.
player_notes.chet holmgren: Described as looking 'defeated' heading into Game 5. Possible minutes or role concern worth monitoring — fade if body language/effort concerns persist.
player_notes.de'aaron fox: Profiled as Spurs' veteran closer/leader. Elevated role and usage expected in a pivotal Game 5.

game_notes.OKC vs SAS: Jalen Williams' availability is unresolved after missing time in Game 4 — his status is critical for Thunder prop and rotation decisions. Thunder had their worst offensive game in years without him.

suggested_context_updates.player_or_team: NYK vs CLE — Eastern Conference Finals
suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: The season context document contains no ECF series diary or bracket update. The last documented series results show R1 completed, with CLE and NYK both advancing. The playoff bracket section shows CLE (4-seed) and NYK (3-seed) as playoff teams but contains no R2 or ECF matchup documentation.
suggested_context_updates.conflict: Multiple news items confirm the Knicks have swept the Cavaliers in the Eastern Conference Finals (4-0), winning Game 4 on May 25 by a score of 130-93, and have advanced to the NBA Finals for the first time since 1999. Karl-Anthony Towns had 19 points and 14 rebounds in the clincher. Donovan Mitchell and James Harden have expressed commitment to staying in Cleveland.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Add ECF series result: NYK defeats CLE 4-0. Update playoff bracket to reflect NYK as the East representative in the NBA Finals. Add ECF diary entries for Games 1-4. Note that CLE is eliminated. Mitchell and Harden expressed desire to remain together in Cleveland (offseason context).

suggested_context_updates.player_or_team: OKC vs SAS — Western Conference Finals
suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: The season context document contains no WCF series diary. The playoff bracket shows OKC (1-seed West) and SAS (2-seed West) as playoff teams but contains no R2 or WCF matchup documentation.
suggested_context_updates.conflict: Multiple news items confirm OKC vs SAS is currently tied 2-2 in the Western Conference Finals, with SAS winning Game 4 on May 25 by a score of 103-82. Wembanyama scored 33 points, 8 rebounds, 5 assists, and 3 blocks. The Thunder held to their worst offensive performance in years. Game 5 is upcoming. Reports note Jalen Williams and Ajay Mitchell were both absent for OKC in Game 4, with Windhorst raising alarm bells about OKC's performance without them.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Add WCF series diary: SAS vs OKC tied 2-2. Add Game 4 result (SAS 103-82). Update Jalen Williams injury status — he remains OUT for WCF (hamstring, now also Ajay Mitchell absent). Add Wembanyama G4 stat line (33/8/5/3blk). Note series is at Game 5 with OKC no longer having home-court advantage in the series narrative.

suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: R2 key note states: 'Jalen Williams' hamstring recovery timeline is the primary OKC health variable heading into the next round — his return would restore their ceiling to its highest level.' The R1 OKC-PHX diary notes Williams went out in G2 with a left hamstring and was OUT through the series sweep.
suggested_context_updates.conflict: Today's news confirms Williams remains OUT in the WCF (Game 4 vs SAS), and Windhorst specifically calls out OKC playing without 'Jalen Williams and Ajay Mitchell' as a concern. Williams has not returned as of WCF Game 4 (May 25).
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Update Jalen Williams status: still OUT in WCF as of Game 4 (May 25). Has not returned from hamstring injury. Also flag Ajay Mitchell as an additional OKC absence not previously documented in the injury landscape.

suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: Ajay Mitchell is not mentioned anywhere in the season context document's injury landscape or player notes.
suggested_context_updates.conflict: Windhorst's report specifically names Ajay Mitchell as OUT alongside Jalen Williams for OKC in the WCF, describing both absences as alarm-bell concerns for the Thunder.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Add Ajay Mitchell (OKC) to the playoff injury landscape as OUT for WCF Game 4 (May 25). Note his absence alongside Williams is flagged by Windhorst as a significant OKC concern heading into Game 5.

Wembanyama missed unanimous first-team by one vote and was also named First-Team All-NBA. Luka Doncic and Cade Cunningham also made First-Team All-NBA per the headline.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Add 2025-26 All-NBA First Team: SGA (unanimous), Jokic (unanimous), Wembanyama, Doncic, Cunningham. This is context/color information; no prop-relevant facts are directly contradicted, but the document should reflect current awards for completeness.
